---
title: "STAT4540_HW4"
author: "John Dennis"
format: pdf
---

```{r,echo=FALSE}
library(astsa)
```

#### Problem 1

Considering the given $AR(2)$, $X_t = X_{t-2}+W_t$ we can find the roots by grouping and solving for the roots:

$$
\begin{gathered}
X_t = X_{t-2}+W_t \\
X_t - X_{t-2} = W_t \\
(1-B^2)X_t = W_t \\ 
\end{gathered}
$$

Once we're here we can solve for the roots by setting $\phi(z)=0$. Note that we replace B with $z \in \mathbb{C}$:

$$
\begin{gathered}
1-z^2 = 0 \\
z^2 = 1 \implies \boxed{z = \pm1}
\end{gathered}
$$

And because $\pm 1$ doesn't fall outside the unit circle, this $AR(2)$ process is not causal.

#### Problem 2
